tasks_completed:
Built baseline RAG pipeline using LangChain and FAISS.

Architecture:
  - Embedding model: sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2
  - Vector store: FAISS with L2 index
  - LLM: Qwen3-8B served via local vLLM endpoint
  - Retriever: top-k=5 dense retrieval

Baseline Retrieval Accuracy (100 test queries):
  - Top-1 accuracy: 72.3%
  - Top-5 accuracy: 89.1%
  - Mean Reciprocal Rank (MRR): 0.74

LangChain orchestration and API Development skills were central to this setup. The vLLM serving endpoint was designed for low-latency batch inference.

tasks_completed:
Converted raw kinematics QA dataset into instruction-tuning format.

Data Preprocessing Steps:
  1. Removed 214 duplicate entries from 3,800 raw samples
  2. Filtered out samples with incomplete answer fields (removed 87 samples)
  3. Normalized question formatting — stripped HTML tags, fixed encoding issues
  4. Applied train/val/test split: 80/10/10 → 3,199 / 400 / 100 samples

Instruction Format Used:
  System: "You are a step-by-step physics tutor..."
  User: "<problem_statement>"
  Assistant: "<step_wise_solution>"

Python scripting skills were applied throughout the pipeline.
next_tasks: Begin LoRA finetuning run with rank 16, learning rate 2e-4, 3 epochs.

tasks_completed:
Applied comprehensive data preprocessing to normalize inputs across benchmarks.

  1. Resized all images to model-native resolution (448x448 for InternVL2, 336x336 for LLaVA)

  3. Fixed 312 corrupted image entries in TextVQA (replaced with placeholder)

tasks_completed:
Evaluated mBERT and XLM-R on XNLI across all 6 languages.

XNLI Results (Accuracy):
  Language    mBERT   XLM-R   Delta
  English:    82.1%   86.3%   +4.2%
  Hindi:      74.3%   79.8%   +5.5%
  Bengali:    69.2%   74.1%   +4.9%
  Tamil:      61.4%   67.2%   +5.8%
  Marathi:    63.8%   69.1%   +5.3%
  Telugu:     60.1%   65.9%   +5.8%
  Average:    68.5%   73.7%   +5.2%

Statistical Analysis: Paired t-test across all language pairs — XLM-R improvement is significant (p < 0.01) for all 6 languages. Effect size (Cohen's d) ranges from 0.41 to 0.67.

Statistical Analysis and Multilingual AI skills were both applied here.
next_tasks: Run TyDi QA evaluation. Investigate why low-resource languages show larger gaps.

tasks_completed:
Evaluated XLM-R on TyDi QA. Conducted deep linguistic analysis of performance gaps.

TyDi QA Results (F1 Score):
  Language    F1      Notes
  English:    72.4    Strong performance
  Hindi:      61.8    Moderate — good transliteration handling
  Bengali:    51.8    Notable drop — complex inflection patterns
  Tamil:      44.2    Significant gap — agglutinative morphology
  Marathi:    47.6    Similar to Bengali
  Telugu:     43.1    Lowest — limited pretraining data

Root Cause Analysis:
  Low-resource languages (Tamil, Telugu, Marathi) have 10–50x less pretraining data in XLM-R compared to English. Morphological complexity compounds this.

Multilingual AI and NLP skills applied. Findings documented in detailed analysis report.
next_tasks: Collect additional training data for Tamil and Telugu to address data scarcity.

tasks_completed:
Ran hybrid retrieval experiments combining BM25 (lexical) and dense embeddings.

Hybrid Retrieval Results:
  Method              NDCG@10   MRR    Latency
  Dense only:         0.74      0.88   430ms
  BM25 only:          0.61      0.76   35ms
  Hybrid (α=0.3):     0.79      0.91   465ms  ← best
  Hybrid (α=0.5):     0.77      0.90   465ms
  Hybrid (α=0.7):     0.75      0.89   465ms

Optimal α=0.3 (30% BM25, 70% dense). Hybrid outperforms dense-only by +0.05 NDCG@10.

Information Retrieval and SQL skills applied to systematically track and query all experiment results.
next_tasks: Implement HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings) for query expansion.

tasks_completed:
Designed and executed evaluation pipeline using GPT-5.1 as LLM judge.

  - Correctness:          4.62 / 5.0
  - Pedagogical Quality:  3.88 / 5.0
  - Feedback Relevance:   4.04 / 5.0
  - Next-Step Clarity:    3.91 / 5.0

Evaluation & Benchmarking and Prompt Engineering skills were applied to design the judge rubric.

tasks_completed:
Evaluated Whisper-medium on out-of-domain phone-call recordings.

Phone-Call Test Set:
  - 200 utterances from customer support calls (8kHz, upsampled to 16kHz)
  - Different acoustic conditions from training data

Out-of-Domain Results:
  Whisper-medium (finetuned):  WER = 21.3%  ← significant domain gap
  Whisper-medium (base):       WER = 24.8%
  Whisper-large-v3 (base):     WER = 17.1%

Root Cause:
  Training data was clean studio speech — model has not seen telephony channel noise, codec artifacts, or cross-talk.

tasks_completed:
Analyzed INT8 quantized Qwen3.5-2B vs full-precision model on tutoring pipeline.

Performance Comparison:
                        2B Full   2B INT8   Delta
  Correctness:          4.71      4.62      -0.09
  Pedagogical Quality:  3.88      3.80      -0.08
  Feedback Relevance:   4.04      3.95      -0.09
  Next-Step Clarity:    4.12      3.87      -0.25  ← largest drop
  Error Recovery:       3.91      3.57      -0.34  ← largest drop

Latency Comparison:
  - Full 2B:  ~9s per step
  - INT8 2B:  ~2.1s per step (4.3x faster)

Root Cause Analysis:
  INT8 quantization compresses small weight magnitudes — reduces overconfidence but degrades recovery reasoning in complex multi-step scenarios.

tasks_completed:

  Language    F1      Notes
  English:    72.4    Strong performance
  Hindi:      61.8    Moderate — good transliteration handling
  Bengali:    51.8    Notable drop — complex inflection patterns
  Tamil:      44.2    Significant gap — agglutinative morphology
  Marathi:    47.6    Similar to Bengali
  Telugu:     43.1    Lowest — limited pretraining data

Root Cause Analysis:
  Low-resource languages (Tamil, Telugu, Marathi) have 10–50x less pretraining data in XLM-R compared to English. Morphological complexity compounds this.

tasks_completed:
Finetuned InternVL2-8B on medical imaging QA dataset using LoRA.

Finetuning Configuration:
  - LoRA rank: 16, alpha: 32, dropout: 0.05
  - Target modules: q_proj, v_proj, k_proj
  - Learning rate: 1e-4 with cosine decay
  - Epochs: 3, batch size: 4

Results (held-out test set, 40 samples):
  Before finetuning:  54.0% overall,  14.3% hard
  After finetuning:   71.5% overall,  42.9% hard  ← significant improvement

Weights & Biases tracked all runs. PyTorch and Model Finetuning skills applied throughout.

tasks_completed:
Ran failure analysis on InternVL2 vs GPT-4V performance gap on medical QA.

Analysis Methodology:
  - Manually reviewed 46 questions where GPT-4V was correct and InternVL2 was wrong
  - Categorized failure types

Failure Mode Distribution:
  Type                              Count   % of failures
  Small object / lesion detection:  18      39.1%
  OCR in dense scan text:           12      26.1%
  Severity scale interpretation:     9      19.6%
  Multi-image reasoning:             7      15.2%

Key Insight:
  InternVL2 struggles most with small-scale spatial reasoning in dense medical images. This is an architecture-level limitation not addressable by prompt tuning alone.

Problem Solving skills applied to design a systematic failure categorization framework.
next_tasks: Incorporate failure analysis findings into final report.

tasks_completed:
Collected and analyzed 2,000 Hinglish (Hindi-English code-switched) samples.

Data Collection:
  - Scraped from Twitter/X using Hindi + English hashtags common in Indian discourse
  - Annotated with: language per token (Hindi/English/Other), sentiment, topic

Baseline Model Performance on Hinglish:
  Model           F1 (NLI)   Notes
  XLM-R:          39.2%      Poor — code-switching not in pretraining distribution
  mBERT:          36.8%      Slightly worse
  GPT-5.1 (few-shot): 58.4%  Strong, handles switching well in context

Key Challenge:
  Standard tokenizers split Hinglish words incorrectly — "karna" (करना) often tokenized as Hindi UNK when written in Latin script.

Communication skills used to present these findings clearly to the team and get alignment on next steps.
next_tasks: Build code-switching classifier and fine-tune for Hinglish NLU.

tasks_completed:
Implemented query result caching using Redis to reduce redundant LLM calls.

Cache Design:
  - Key: SHA256 hash of (query + top-5 retrieved doc IDs)
  - TTL: 1 hour
  - Cache hit rate on test set: 34% (many repeated queries in evaluation)

Performance with Caching:
  - Cache hit latency:  ~18ms
  - Cache miss latency: ~873ms (no change)
  - Effective avg latency (with 34% hit rate): ~593ms

Information Retrieval, API Development, and Problem Solving skills applied to design the caching layer correctly without stale result issues.
next_tasks: Run full 1,000-query benchmark and prepare final pipeline report.
